commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Therefore rather, brethren, give diligence to make your calling and election sure: for if you do these things, you shall never fall: By good works you may make sure without diving into the hidden mysteries of predestination You shall not sin at any time. These words evidently suppose, that the graces and assistances of God will not be wanting; for it would be in vain to command, unless a man had both free will and capacity to perform. But, as it follows, these helps shall be abundantly ministered to you.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
